name: nonzero_index_zero_result
code:
proof fn phi_1_nonzero_index_zero_result(i: usize)
    requires
        0 < i < NUM_PAGES,
    ensures
        spec_page_index2page_ptr(i) == 0,
{
}
reason: A nonzero page index mapping to pointer 0 would indicate arithmetic overflow or a collapsed mapping

name: max_index_wraps_below_input
code:
proof fn phi_2_max_index_wraps_below_input(i: usize)
    requires
        i == (NUM_PAGES - 1) as usize,
    ensures
        spec_page_index2page_ptr(i) < i,
{
}
reason: At the maximum valid index the result wrapping below the index itself would indicate unsigned overflow in the multiplication

name: non_injective_mapping
code:
proof fn phi_3_non_injective_mapping(i: usize, j: usize)
    requires
        0 <= i < NUM_PAGES,
        0 <= j < NUM_PAGES,
        i != j,
    ensures
        spec_page_index2page_ptr(i) == spec_page_index2page_ptr(j),
{
}
reason: Two distinct page indices producing the same pointer would make the mapping ambiguous and corrupt page identity

name: result_exceeds_max_usize
code:
proof fn phi_4_result_exceeds_max_usize(i: usize)
    requires
        0 <= i < NUM_PAGES,
    ensures
        spec_page_index2page_ptr(i) > MAX_USIZE as usize,
{
}
reason: A page pointer exceeding the declared MAX_USIZE bound would violate the system's memory model invariant

name: not_page_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_not_page_aligned(i: usize)
    requires
        0 <= i < NUM_PAGES,
    ensures
        spec_page_index2page_ptr(i) % 4096 != 0,
{
}
reason: A page pointer that is not 4096-aligned would violate the fundamental page-alignment invariant required by the memory subsystem
